explanation: Four concurrent workers + the live API + seed_round all write the same sqlite file. Connections were opened WITHOUT WAL journal mode and WITHOUT a busy_timeout, so writers collided and raised 'database is locked'. execute_task survived (the lease-leak guard caught it and released the lease), but seed_round's UPDATE was UNGUARDED -- the exception escaped the soak loop and killed the process.
evidence: dozens of 'execute body raised: database is locked' lines, then an unhandled OperationalError traceback at soak_runner.py:97
what_worked: The lease-leak guard from 23a738d0 held: every locked task still released its lease. Leaked leases at crash: 0
why_shorter_tests_missed_it: Contention scales with sustained concurrent write volume. 38 rounds x 4 workers was enough; a 4-round smoke was not.
